hero.subhead: A data- and AI-driven platform to understand, compare, and act on Brazil's educational indicators.

ideb.name: IDEB
ideb.desc: Basic Education Development Index. Combines pass rate and SAEB score on a 0–10 scale. National average: ~4.5.

saeb.name: SAEB
saeb.desc: Standardized national assessment for 5th and 9th grade and high school seniors. One of the two inputs for IDEB.

qedu.name: QEDU
qedu.desc: Education data platform aggregating INEP microdata. Used for enrollment, IDEB, and dropout rates at school level.

pnad.name: PNAD
pnad.desc: National Household Sample Survey. Provides income, literacy, and race/gender education breakdowns.

atlas.name: Atlas Brasil
atlas.desc: Municipal Human Development Index database. Used to contextualize socioeconomic conditions in equity analysis.

equityIndex.name: Equity Index
equityIndex.desc: Composite of 6 dimensions: racial gap, gender gap, literacy rate, dropout rate, urban/rural gap, and income decile.

mlRisk.name: ML Risk v0.6
mlRisk.desc: Gradient Boosted Trees model (LightGBM) trained on 14 municipal indicators. Score > 0.65 indicates critical dropout risk.

kpi.idebTooltip: Weighted average of IDEB (EF + EM) across all states. Source: INEP 2023.
kpi.statesTooltip: States with complete IDEB, PNAD, and School Census data available.
kpi.riskTooltip: Municipalities with a dropout risk score > 0.65 from the LightGBM v0.6 model.

risk.title: Cities with most Schools At Risk
risk.mlRiskTooltip: LightGBM model trained on 14 municipal indicators. Score 0–1; values above 0.65 indicate critical school dropout risk.

radar.title: Equity Index

tooltips.racial: Gap between white and Black/Brown student outcomes (PNAD/IBGE).
tooltips.gender: Gender parity index: 1.0 = equal years of schooling for men and women.
tooltips.literacy: % of population aged 15+ who can read and write (PNAD/IBGE).
tooltips.ruralUrban: IDEB difference between urban and rural schools.
tooltips.income: IDEB gap between the highest and lowest income deciles.

radar.titleTooltip: Each axis ranges from 0 to 1. Values close to 1.0 indicate high equity or parity. Values below 0.8 signal significant inequality. The 6 axes measure: racial gap (IDHM-E), gender parity (years of schooling), literacy, school dropout, urban/rural gap, and income gap.

suggestions.compareNE: Compare SE to the Northeast average
suggestions.ideb10y: IDEB evolution over the last 10 years
suggestions.risk: Which municipalities are most at risk?
suggestions.vsJapan: How does Brazil compare to Japan and Finland?
suggestions.dropoutCause: Why do students drop out of school?

empty.welcome: Hello! Choose a suggestion below or ask your question.
